violations.description: File is 365 lines, exceeding maintainability target and making review/debug slower.
violations.suggestion: Split by behavior: signature refusal, mapping success, replay-safe dedupe, unmapped refusal.

violations.description: File is 510 lines with multiple concerns in one suite.
violations.suggestion: Split into focused suites (signature, provider fallback, public routing, ambiguity, conflict).

violations.description: File is 423 lines, increasing cognitive load and change risk.
violations.suggestion: Refactor into smaller e2e suites grouped by happy-path, replay-safe, and refusal scenarios.

violations.description: Signature/header and payload builder logic is duplicated across API/E2E ATDD and automate files.
violations.suggestion: Extract shared E1 webhook test helpers under tests/support/helpers/connectShyftWebhookTestHelpers.ts.

violations.description: Entire ATDD E2E suite is currently skipped, which increases drift risk over time.
violations.suggestion: Re-enable incrementally or quarantine with explicit TODO + gate ownership metadata.
violations.code_snippet: test.skip('[P0] end-to-end ingress journey accepts valid signed webhook ...')

recommendations: Split large E1 specs into behavior-focused files under 300 lines
Extract duplicated signing/payload utilities into shared support helpers
Re-enable or consciously retire skipped ATDD E2E scenarios with ownership
summary: Maintainability is the weakest dimension due to oversized files and repeated helper logic.
